mnemonics: HOT POTATO for Queue: First In First Out (FIFO) - like the first person to get a hot potato is the first one to pass it on.
STACK OF PLATES for Stack: Last In First Out (LIFO) - you always take the top plate, which was the last one placed.
keyPoints: A Queue is a linear data structure that follows FIFO (First-In-First-Out) principle.
A Stack is a linear data structure that follows LIFO (Last-In-First-Out) principle.
Common operations for both include Insertion (Enqueue/Push), Deletion (Dequeue/Pop), and checking if empty/full.
commonMistakes: Attempting to pop/dequeue from an empty stack/queue, causing an underflow error.
Forgetting to check for stack overflow when pushing onto a fixed-size stack.
Confusing the order of operations, e.g., trying to access the middle element directly in a stack, which violates its LIFO principle.
